# TCAT Wiki

> Documentation for transportation accessibility tools and data developed by the
> Taskar Center for Accessible Technology (TCAT) at the University of Washington.
> This wiki covers OS-CONNECT (Washington State's connected pedestrian network
> dataset), TDEI (the platform for storing and serving that data), OpenSidewalks
> (the data schema/standard), AccessMap (accessible pedestrian routing), Walksheds
> (pedestrian network analysis), Workspaces (dataset editing tools), and AVIV
> ScoutRoute (a crowdsourcing mobile app for pedestrian data collection).

TCAT develops open-source tools and data infrastructure to improve pedestrian
accessibility and mobility for people with disabilities. The core dataset is
OS-CONNECT — a high-resolution connected pedestrian network (sidewalks, crossings,
curb ramps, and related features) for Washington State, stored and served via the
TDEI platform using the OpenSidewalks schema.

Key acronyms:

- **TCAT** — Taskar Center for Accessible Technology (at the University of Washington)
- **OSW** — OpenSidewalks: the open pedestrian network data standard/schema
- **TDEI** — Transportation Data Exchange Initiative: the platform and API infrastructure for transportation accessibility data
- **OS-CONNECT** — Washington State's connected pedestrian network dataset (structured in the OSW schema)
- **ASR** — AVIV ScoutRoute: a mobile crowdsourcing app for pedestrian data collection

Architecture: data generation tools (AVIV ScoutRoute, Rapid editor, Prophet) feed
into TDEI Core (ingestion, validation, metadata, quality checks, access control,
publishing, APIs), and data flows from there to consumer apps (AccessMap, Walksheds,
Audiom, Conveyal).

## Key Pages

- [Site Overview](https://taskarcenteratuw.github.io/tcat-wiki/): Project map, user journeys, and architecture overview
- [Guides List](https://taskarcenteratuw.github.io/tcat-wiki/guides-list/): Complete index of all guides on the TCAT Wiki
- [OS-CONNECT](https://taskarcenteratuw.github.io/tcat-wiki/os-connect/): Washington State connected pedestrian network dataset
- [TDEI](https://taskarcenteratuw.github.io/tcat-wiki/tdei/): Platform infrastructure for transportation accessibility data
- [OpenSidewalks](https://taskarcenteratuw.github.io/tcat-wiki/opensidewalks/): Open pedestrian network data standard and schema
- [AccessMap](https://taskarcenteratuw.github.io/tcat-wiki/accessmap/): Accessibility-focused multimodal routing web app
- [Walksheds](https://taskarcenteratuw.github.io/tcat-wiki/walksheds/): Pedestrian network analysis and access-to-destinations tool
- [Workspaces](https://taskarcenteratuw.github.io/tcat-wiki/workspaces/): TDEI dataset editing platform
- [AVIV ScoutRoute](https://taskarcenteratuw.github.io/tcat-wiki/aviv-scoutroute/): Mobile crowdsourcing app for pedestrian data collection

## OS-CONNECT

- [OS-CONNECT Overview](https://taskarcenteratuw.github.io/tcat-wiki/os-connect/): High-resolution connected pedestrian network for Washington State (320+ cities and counties)
- [OS-CONNECT Data Viewer User Manual](https://taskarcenteratuw.github.io/tcat-wiki/os-connect/data-viewer/user-manual/): How to use the map-based OS-CONNECT Data Viewer (no GIS required)
- [QA/QC Reports User Manual](https://taskarcenteratuw.github.io/tcat-wiki/os-connect/qa-qc/user-manual/): How to read and interpret QA/QC jurisdiction snapshot reports

## TDEI

- [TDEI Overview](https://taskarcenteratuw.github.io/tcat-wiki/tdei/): Platform structure, user roles (data consumer, point of contact, data publisher), and data lifecycle
- [TDEI Portal User Manual](https://taskarcenteratuw.github.io/tcat-wiki/tdei/portal/user-manual/): Account registration, API keys, data downloads, and portal access
- [TDEI Core](https://taskarcenteratuw.github.io/tcat-wiki/tdei/tdei-core/): Core infrastructure documentation

## OpenSidewalks Schema

- [OpenSidewalks Overview](https://taskarcenteratuw.github.io/tcat-wiki/opensidewalks/): Initiative overview and OSM US Tasking Manager usage
- [OpenSidewalks Schema](https://taskarcenteratuw.github.io/tcat-wiki/opensidewalks/schema/): Data standard for pedestrian networks (nodes, edges, zones, points, lines, polygons)
- [Core Edges in OSW](https://taskarcenteratuw.github.io/tcat-wiki/opensidewalks/schema/core-edges-in-osw/): Guide to representing sidewalks, crossings, and connections in the OSW schema
- [Custom Points in OSW](https://taskarcenteratuw.github.io/tcat-wiki/opensidewalks/schema/custom-points-in-osw/): Guide to representing curb ramps and other point features in OSW
- [OpenSidewalks in OSM](https://taskarcenteratuw.github.io/tcat-wiki/opensidewalks/schema/osw-in-osm/): Guidelines for contributing OpenSidewalks data via OpenStreetMap
- [OSM US Tasking Manager](https://taskarcenteratuw.github.io/tcat-wiki/opensidewalks/tasking-manager/): Coordinated community mapping project information
- [Tasking Manager Project List](https://taskarcenteratuw.github.io/tcat-wiki/opensidewalks/tasking-manager/project-list/): Active and completed OpenSidewalks mapping projects

## Workspaces & Editing

- [Workspaces Overview](https://taskarcenteratuw.github.io/tcat-wiki/workspaces/): TDEI Workspaces platform for editing pedestrian datasets (supports Rapid, JOSM, and AVIV ScoutRoute)
- [Rapid Editor User Manual](https://taskarcenteratuw.github.io/tcat-wiki/rapid/user-manual/): How to use the Rapid (iD-based) editor within Workspaces
- [JOSM Settings Guide](https://taskarcenteratuw.github.io/tcat-wiki/josm/workspaces-josm-settings/): JOSM configuration for editing data in TDEI Workspaces
- [AVIV ScoutRoute User Manual](https://taskarcenteratuw.github.io/tcat-wiki/aviv-scoutroute/user-manual/): How to use the AVIV ScoutRoute mobile app to contribute pedestrian data
- [AVIV ScoutRoute Quest Definition Guides](https://taskarcenteratuw.github.io/tcat-wiki/aviv-scoutroute/quests/): How to create and configure AVIV ScoutRoute quests (Long Form Quest Definition format)

## AccessMap & Walksheds

- [AccessMap User Manual](https://taskarcenteratuw.github.io/tcat-wiki/accessmap/user-manual/): How to use AccessMap for accessible, customized pedestrian routing
- [Walksheds User Manual](https://taskarcenteratuw.github.io/tcat-wiki/walksheds/user-manual/): How to use the TDEI Walksheds tool for scenario planning and infrastructure prioritization

## Optional

- [Abbreviations Glossary](https://taskarcenteratuw.github.io/tcat-wiki/includes/abbreviations/): Full glossary of 100+ acronyms and abbreviations used across TCAT tools and documentation
- [Events](https://taskarcenteratuw.github.io/tcat-wiki/events/): Organized editing events (mapathons) and post-event reports
- [Tasking Manager User Manual](https://taskarcenteratuw.github.io/tcat-wiki/opensidewalks/tasking-manager/user-manual/): How to participate in OpenSidewalks organized editing on the OSM US Tasking Manager
